title: I Wanted to Protect the Town
overview: What can a single Tamer do against an entire army of 10,000 monsters with just a wolf, a dryad and a handful of slimes?

title: Trying Out a Party
overview: Yuji’s party members don’t have a high opinion of Tamers, but his tracking skills are too valuable to pass up. Maybe Yuji can change their minds once they see him in action.

title: I Meant Too Strong
overview: Yuji overdoes everything, even when he's trying not to. But when a dragon appears underneath the calamitous blue moon, the Tamer may be the only one who can prevent disaster.

title: Truly Unusual Situations
overview: A dragon is a formidable opponent. That's precisely why Yuji needs to get up close and personal to fight it with... a dagger!?

title: That Cold Looked Troubling
overview: A hard freeze means a high demand for firewood. It's time for Yuji to use his overpowered skills for a more mundane purpose, especially since delicious food is involved!

title: Infiltrating an Ominous Village
overview: The bad news? There's something sinister going on in that remote village. The good news? The cult stationed there has no idea Yuji is on to them.

title: Assassins in Pursuit
overview: Yuuji has a price on his head and two assassins are after him. He could easily defeat them, but that would only make him a bigger target. This calls for a more unique approach.

title: We Got Some Monster Armor
overview: If Yuji wants quality armor, he’ll need quality materials. And that means hunting a rare monster.

title: We Fought a Fire Dragon
overview: Yuji must now protect the town from the kaiju-level threat of a rampaging Fire Dragon.

title: Didn’t Seem Like a Good Time for That
overview: According to legend, an emissary is supposed to stop the Blue Moon of Salvation’s leader. Steyl is now convinced that Yuji is the one he’s been waiting for.

title: The Revelation Came True
overview: The battle between Yuji and Walter begins in earnest, but that is only the beginning.

title: My Isekai Life
overview: Yuji may be strong, but he can’t fight the enemy and defend all the towns at once. If he wants to win, he needs to place his trust in all the friends he made along the way.
